With <code>search-workers</code> > 0` (the default), the timeout is enforced preemptively</li> <li><code>RETURN</code> (default) — return best-effort partial results, without enforcing strictness during post-processing</li> <li><code>RETURN_STRICT</code> (new) — return best-effort partial results while enforcing the timeout through the post-processing (result) pipeline</li> </ul> </li> class="issue-link js-issue-link" data-error-text="Failed to load title" data-id="4827374392" data-permission-text="Title is private" data-url="https://github.com/RedisJSON/RedisJSON/issues/1618" data-hovercard-type="pull_request" data-hovercard-url="/RedisJSON/RedisJSON/pull/1618/hovercard" href="https://github.com/RedisJSON/RedisJSON/pull/1618">RedisJSON/RedisJSON#1618</a> JSONPath extensions (MOD-16274, MOD-16275): <ul> <li>Projection expressions at the top level of a JSONPath query</li> <li><code>==</code> and <code>!=</code> can now compare any literal, including array and object literals</li> <li>Filter negation operator: <code>!</code></li> <li><code>size</code>/<code>sizeof</code> and <code>empty</code> operators on string, array, object, and nodelist</li> <li><code>in</code> and <code>nin</code> operators: membership test on an array and nodelist</li> <li>Operators on numbers: binary <code>-</code>, <code>+</code>, <code>*</code>, <code>/</code>, <code>%</code>, and unary <code>-</code> and <code>+</code></li> <li>Operator on object: <code>~</code></li> <li><code>length()</code> function on array, object, and string</li> <li>Functions on number: <code>abs()</code>, <code>ceiling()</code>, <code>floor()</code></li> <li>Functions on string: <code>match()</code>, <code>search()</code></li> <li>Strings concatenation with <code>concat()</code></li> <li>Functions on array: <code>first()</code>, <code>last()</code>, <code>index()</code>, <code>append()</code></li> <li>Aggregation functions on array: <code>min()</code>, <code>max()</code>, <code>avg()</code>, <code>sum()</code>, <code>stddev()</code></li> <li>Function on object: <code>keys()</code></li> <li>Function on nodelist: <code>count()</code></li> <li>Function on nodelist with exactly one node: <code>value()</code></li> <li>Relations functions on array and nodelist: <code>subsetof()</code>, <code>anyof()</code>, <code>noneof()</code></li> </ul> </li> <li><a
